title: Toddalia megapotamica
intro: Toddalia megapotamica is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Toddalia megapotamica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Toddalia megapotamica include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
taxonomy: Toddalia megapotamica is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of South America. This species is also known as the 'pepper wort' due to its pungent odor and flavor. Toddalia megapotamica is classified under the genus Toddalia, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The plant is commonly found in humid and subtropical regions.
